growth_records.medium: Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer with Pseudomonas sp. prey (predation batch)

up.units: prey cells consumed per predator cell per h
up.method: calculated q_prey = mu_Y/Ypredator = 0.266 / 1.95; prey consumption measured by flow cytometry

yields.Yxs: 1.95 B. bacteriovorus cells per Pseudomonas sp. cell (predator yield, experimental)

growth_records.cite: Ogundero et al. Developing Lotka-Volterra Based Models to Describe Bdellovibrio Predation in a Batch and Chemostat Experimental System. Environmental Microbiology Reports (2025). DOI:10.1111/1758-2229.70141. [PMC12229818]
growth_records.snippet: Maximum predator growth rate 0.266 h-1 ... 0.244 h-1; Predator yield 1.95 B. bacteriovorus cells/Pseudomonas sp. cell; Prey saturation constant 5.59 x 10^7 cells/mL (Holling II) / 2.74 x 10^15 cells^2/mL^2 (Holling III)
growth_records.notes: Bdellovibrio bacteriovorus HD100 (predator) grown by predation on Pseudomonas sp. in Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer, batch, 30C, 72 h; prey and predator enumerated by flow cytometry. EXPERIMENTAL kinetic parameters (Table 1, Lineweaver-Burk / regression fits): maximum predator specific growth rate mu_Y = 0.266 h-1 (Holling type II fit) or 0.244 h-1 (Holling type III fit); predator yield Y = 1.95 predator cells per prey cell consumed; prey half-saturation constant Kx = 5.59e7 prey cells/mL (Holling II) or 2.74e15 cells^2/mL^2 (Holling III). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.266 = 2.606 h (calculated, using Holling II mu). Specific prey-consumption (uptake) rate q_prey = mu_Y/Ypredator = 0.266 h-1 / 1.95 predator/prey = 0.1364 prey cells per predator cell per h (calculated; here the consumed 'substrate' is prey biomass). pH 7.6 from Ca/Mg-HEPES buffer (25 mM HEPES, 2 mM CaCl2, 3 mM MgCl2, pH 7.6).

growth_records.strain: B. bacteriovorus HD100 (DSM 50701) / Pseudomonas sp. DSM 50906
growth_records.cite: Ogundero et al. Developing Lotka-Volterra Based Models to Describe Bdellovibrio Predation in a Batch and Chemostat Experimental System. Environmental Microbiology Reports (2025). DOI:10.1111/1758-2229.70141. [PMC12229818]
growth_records.snippet: a chemostat was operated for 168 h using the Applikon my-Control bioreactor ... minimal medium (HEPES buffer with 2 mM calcium chloride, 3 mM magnesium chloride, 0.05% casein hydrolysate, and 25 mg/L glucose) was continuously supplied. Inle
growth_records.notes: Co-culture continuous (chemostat) experiment, Run 1: dilution rate D = 0.17 h-1, 30C, 168 h, influent glucose 25 mg/L, casein hydrolysate 0.05%, in Ca/Mg-HEPES. This is one of the first experimental realisations of Bdellovibrio predator-prey oscillations in a chemostat; predator and prey populations oscillate out of phase, so no single steady-state growth rate applies (at true steady state mu would equal D = 0.17 h-1, but the system is oscillatory/limit-cycle). Species-specific uptake/secretion rates not reported; effluent prey/predator/glucose measured as time series (flow cytometry + Sigma glucose kit) but numerical rates not tabulated. Temperature/pH inherited from Ca/Mg-HEPES base (30C, pH 7.6).

growth_records.strain: B. bacteriovorus HD100 (DSM 50701) / Pseudomonas sp. DSM 50906
growth_records.medium: Continuous minimal medium (chemostat Run 2, biofilm quantification)
growth_records.cite: Ogundero et al. Developing Lotka-Volterra Based Models to Describe Bdellovibrio Predation in a Batch and Chemostat Experimental System. Environmental Microbiology Reports (2025). DOI:10.1111/1758-2229.70141. [PMC12229818]
growth_records.snippet: the chemostat was operated again for 72 h with the addition of sterile glass slides ... B. bacteriovorus Planktonic 1.23E+09; Pseudomonas sp. 5.57E+10; B. bacteriovorus Biofilm 1.19E+09; Pseudomonas sp. 4.03E+10
growth_records.notes: Co-culture chemostat Run 2 (D assumed 0.17 h-1 as in Run 1; the paper says 'operated again' with same setup plus glass slides), 30C, 72 h. Table 2 standing cell abundances (flow cytometry): planktonic B. bacteriovorus 1.23e9 cells, planktonic Pseudomonas sp. 5.57e10 cells; biofilm B. bacteriovorus 1.19e9 cells, biofilm Pseudomonas sp. 4.03e10 cells; combined B. bacteriovorus 2.42e9, Pseudomonas 9.60e10. These are endpoint abundances, not rates. Included as a distinct experimental condition documenting biofilm vs planktonic partitioning that explained model overestimation. No specific growth/uptake/secretion rates tabulated. NOTE: dilution rate for Run 2 not explicitly restated; inherited 0.17 h-1 from Run 1 setup.
